task_completion.description: Open the starter Blender file containing the LegRig armature.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender is open and the 3D viewport shows the 'LegRig' armature.

task_completion.description: Add an Inverse Kinematics constraint to the Shin.L bone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Pose Mode, with 'Shin.L' selected, the Bone Constraints properties tab shows an 'Inverse Kinematics' constraint.

task_completion.description: Set the IK Target to Foot_IK.L and Pole Target to Knee_Target.L.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the IK constraint panel, the Target is set to 'LegRig' with Bone 'Foot_IK.L', and the Pole Target is set to 'LegRig' with Bone 'Knee_Target.L'.

task_completion.description: Set the IK Chain Length to 2.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the IK constraint panel, the 'Chain Length' field is set to exactly 2.

task_completion.description: Adjust the Pole Angle so the knee points toward the pole target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Pole Angle' in the IK constraint is adjusted (typically to 90, -90, or 180 degrees), and the knee visually points towards the 'Knee_Target.L' bone.

task_completion.description: Move the Foot_IK.L bone up along the Z-axis to show the active pose.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Foot_IK.L' bone is translated upwards in the 3D viewport, causing the leg (Shin.L and Thigh.L) to bend at the knee, demonstrating a working IK chain.

task_completion.description: Save the project to the specified completed file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender window title bar or a save dialog shows the filename 'leg_rig_completed.blend'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender GUI to configure the rig.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Blender's 3D Viewport, Properties editor, and Pose Mode, rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass manual rigging by running a Python script.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Scripting workspace or Python console is not used to instantly generate the constraints and pose.

integrity.description: The agent progressively built the constraint and posed the armature.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show step-by-step progress: selecting bones, adding the constraint, configuring fields, and moving the IK control bone.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The armature is named 'LegRig'. The IK constraint must be applied to the 'Shin.L' bone. The IK Target must be 'Foot_IK.L' and the Pole Target must be 'Knee_Target.L'. The Chain Length must be exactly 2 to control both the Shin.L and Thigh.L bones. The final file must be saved as '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/leg_rig_completed.blend'. The Foot_IK.L bone must be moved up along the Z-axis to demonstrate the IK constraint working (the leg should bend).
